text.title: Messages API Request, Parameters, and Response Structure
text.summary: Basic request structure, parameters table, response object fields, content block types, and multi-turn conversation pattern.

example.code:
import anthropic
client = anthropic.Anthropic()
message = client.messages.create( model="claude-sonnet-4-20250514", max_tokens=1024, system="You are an expert on Specification-Driven Development. Be concise.", messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What are the key phases of SDD?"}] ) print(message.content[0].text)

list.item: max_tokens: required — max response tokens; cannot be omitted; truncates if too low
messages: required — alternating user/assistant turns; consecutive same-role messages → 400 error
system: optional — set behavior; kept stable across calls for Prompt Caching benefit
stop_sequences: optional — matches on next token boundary; mid-sentence stop is cut silently
metadata: optional — add user_id for abuse detection and cost attribution

rule.statement: Always set max_tokens high enough for expected output — silent truncation (stop_reason="max_tokens") is the most common cause of corrupted downstream data.
rule.rationale: max_tokens is required and easy to set too low; truncation produces parseable but incomplete output that passes format checks and fails semantic ones.

rule.statement: Check stop_reason before accessing content[0].text — if stop_reason is "tool_use", content[0] may be a tool_use block, not text.
rule.rationale: Content blocks are polymorphic; indexing blindly into content[0].text raises AttributeError when the first block is a tool_use or thinking block.

rule.statement: Append the full resp.content list (not just text) as the assistant turn in multi-turn history — tool_use blocks must be preserved or the next turn gets a 400 error.
rule.rationale: The API reconstructs conversation state from message history; missing tool_use blocks make the history inconsistent and cause an API error.

example.code: messages = [ {"role": "user", "content": "What is SDD?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "SDD (Specification-Driven Development) is..."}, {"role": "user", "content": "How does it compare to TDD?"} ] response = client.messages.create(model="claude-sonnet-4-20250514", max_tokens=1024, messages=messages) # Append full content list, not just text messages.append({"role": "assistant", "content": response.content})
